meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Build a Portfolio and Launch Your Career
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about no portfolio. Learn how to leverage Git with AI, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build professional-grade projects that get you hired.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping real-world utility, not just classroom exercises. By integrating AI-assisted development tools like Antigravity and Claude Code into your Git workflow, you can rapidly prototype, document, and deploy production-ready applications, turning your GitHub profile into a high-signal asset that demonstrates professional engineering competence.
content.intro:
The blank repository is the silent killer of student careers. You’ve mastered the theory, completed the courses, and yet, staring at a repository with zero commits feels like standing on the edge of a cliff. The gap between 'tutorial completion' and 'market-ready developer' isn't talent—it's the friction of building, debugging, and documenting real-world software in a structured environment.

This is where the AI-augmented developer thrives. We aren't just writing code; we are orchestrating it. By merging the iterative power of Claude Code with the architectural guidance of Antigravity, you can transform a single idea into a polished, version-controlled product. This isn't just about finishing a project; it's about building a professional narrative that recruiters can't ignore.

steps.title: Architecting the MVP
steps.description: Define the scope of your project using Antigravity to generate a robust technical specification that guides your development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Software Architect. Create a technical project specification for a [Project Name] that solves [Specific Problem]. Include the directory structure, key dependencies, and a prioritized feature list for an MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Create a new project scaffold based on this spec: [Paste Spec]'

steps.title: Configuring Git Workflow
steps.description: Initialize your repository with a professional branching strategy to ensure your commit history tells a story of iterative progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .gitignore file tailored for [Language/Framework] and suggest a Git flow strategy suitable for a single developer building a complex SaaS tool.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write functional code blocks while maintaining high test coverage and clean documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular, testable function for [Feature Name] that adheres to SOLID principles and includes JSDoc/Docstring documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Implement the [Feature Name] module, write unit tests, and explain the logic in the README.md'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Leverage AI to critique your own pull requests, identifying edge cases and security vulnerabilities before merging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a senior-level code review on the following code diff. Identify potential memory leaks, performance bottlenecks, and security risks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Review the current branch against best practices for [Language/Framework] and suggest optimizations'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Finalize your project with professional documentation that showcases your ability to communicate complex systems to stakeholders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional README.md that includes an 'Architecture Overview', 'Setup Instructions', and 'How to Contribute' section for my portfolio.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate a professional README.md based on the project files and push to main'

pro_tips: Treat your commit messages as technical logs; use the Conventional Commits specification to show you understand team-based professional standards.
Never push code without a corresponding test; AI-generated tests are the best way to prove your code actually works to potential employers.
Use Claude Code to generate 'ADR' files (Architecture Decision Records) in your repo to show you think about trade-offs, not just syntax.
Keep your main branch clean; always develop on feature branches to demonstrate you understand the Git lifecycle used in enterprise environments.
Embed a 'Demo' link in your README that points to a live deployment; a clickable app is worth ten times more than a static repo.
content.next_steps: Once your repository is polished, deploy it to Vercel or Netlify, add a professional case study to your personal website, and begin contributing to open-source projects using the same Git-with-AI workflow.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every line of code you push is a step toward your first job in the industry. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
